So the full funnel system that doesn't change the 5w avatar That doesn't change the 3n ads formula of creating ads that doesn't change but things do change based on what Facebook introduced So Facebook introduced CBO it took us some time, but we started using it Facebook introduced the DCO dynamic content optimization and That has proved to be really useful. So this is an example of Three different ways that we set up our warm campaigns So this one here is just a basic list of audiences as you can see Video views people have engaged on Facebook fans, etc. And you can see here that the performance is fairly even across all of them But one of the cool things about how we prefer to use CBO is because these are all in the CBO campaign We're letting Facebook optimize the spend and ROAS and overall is giving us strong performance Now you might look at some of these and say, you know, we should spend more Where the ROAS is good, but the audience sizes are limited So Facebook is making the budget decisions day by day as the audience quality and size changes that we don't have to So we can set the budget at the CBO level Set the create the ads and let Facebook Decide where to put the budget. So this is a standard view So this is a campaign with an ad set with multiple ads running in each In a standard way of setting up a warm audience and these are the warm audiences in play One thing I will say as a reference point is when we create our warm audiences We exclude other audiences from them. So this video views audience here Not only will it exclude people below the warm funnel

## [03:00 - 05:59]

So people that are in the hot audience they've clicked into the site added to cart purchased, etc But we're also Excluding the audiences from each other so that there's no overlap and the spend is clean So in the video views audience, we're excluding people that have engaged for 30 days We're excluding fans and emails same for this So that what happens is each of these audiences are not overlapping and Therefore Facebook is optimizing the spend based on whoever's in each of these subset of audiences. So that's the first thing Now that's a standard way of setting up campaigns as you've seen previously. I do prefer to use DCO But sometimes we we we get a legacy account and therefore it's harder to shift But to give you an example here where we did make the shift and and this is something as a separate FYI on kind of How we like to run promotions when promotions are running. I don't like to In put those promotions into existing campaigns Because of the instability that it causes now for example, this is a short-term promotion That's currently running and it's just started, but it will also end soon so rather than introduce those ads to an existing ad set and and potentially impact and Effect our stability because it all comes back to stability. I create a separate campaign With the same ad sets that I want to promote But it's away from my main campaigns so that when I stop and start this promotional campaign I'm not impacting my business as usual Now one of the downsides that you could kind of look at this and say, you know because they're separate CBOs There is an element of audience lap overlap But over time we found especially with decent spend volumes and decent audience sizes as we've got here There isn't much of a negative impact So what we do is sometimes we'll reduce the spend down for our main CBOs and push more spend into our promotional CBO so the same audience is for warm and Then balance the spend between both and you can see here for example We've just recently launched this. It's got a fairly good row ass, but it's still early on but one of the things about this is we are using the infinity strategy of duplicating the Ad sets and running different copy variants in each of them as well So this one you can see is copy 10 in the little overview. You can see copy 9 copy 8 etc So each of these are fairly similar audiences, but we're using different copy types as angles That way we're giving Facebook the best opportunity to optimize performance for those variants And actually if we do the breakdown report, we can then look down at the ad level and look deeper as to What's working and what's not? So if we go down into this

## [06:00 - 09:05]

Ad set here and look at this ad. So this is a single ad that's running He's doing okay It's just started and then we can go to the breakdown and look at the breakdown of exactly What's performing and where and what it would do especially in a promotion? Period because a you don't have real too much really too much time to Test especially a short term promotion like this. It might be three days five days, etc Is push them into your DCO push your variables in so previously I've discussed three copy variants a couple of image and video variants and headline push them into your DCO And then if you see any bad performers, you can either let Facebook decide what works where Or what I like to do is remove those very much very variants From the ad itself So if you see for example a particular headline isn't working Then you can take that headline out and let it continue running and generally the performance will continue if you're worried about performance you can duplicate that ad set with the same DCO and Fire it up again with your changes made Run them side by side and then let them stabilize and figure out which one's working best and then reduce the budget from one and let the other one You can pause one of them and let the other one run So there's different ways that you can view this and kind of manage this But generally what I like to do is run that as a DCO So you don't have to worry about too much time put into testing Especially on short term campaigns. So that's the second part of just showing you how to Look at your warm audiences now the third thing here, which is quite a cool way of building up social proof Is actually doing your social proofing on further down? Audiences into your funnel So previously we've talked about graduation testing and moving ads into the social proofing part of the process in this particular campaign We've got a page post engagement campaign PPE running on our warm audiences Where we are copying the post IDs into ad sets and letting them build up social proof But we're doing it with friendly Users and why main by friendly is if you imagine doing social proofing Against cold audiences, which most people end up doing and you know, we do as well They don't know your brand or products. They might like the page The post that might engage with the post, but it's not the same as people that have engaged with your ads It's not the same as people have that have Visited your site or even purchased so often we use our Social proofing campaigns further down the funnel. So warm audiences hot audiences But in particular existing customers now you might look it and say well, you know, if I'm running a prospecting ad Do I really want an existing customer to see it? ## [09:05 - 12:05]

I bought it recently. It's great, etc. especially if you've got a high customer satisfaction rating and the cool thing about that is Your prospecting audiences, especially if you're able to or in particular if you're able to share your post ID They'll see the comments. They don't know it's a you know, this is the strategy you're running But they will see the comments from existing customers on your ad and they'll think this is great There's social proofing done right there and this is a way or that many Some businesses build the social proof in their ads by running an ad in prospecting Using a friendly audience so people that bought 14 days after purchase or whatever it is Maybe even doing some cohort analysis and saying find people that bought twice in the last three months Or you know do some lifetime value analysis people that are high lifetime value customers and share the post ID to that audience in your existing customer funnel and The social proof you get on those ads is just so so powerful Because even five or six comments on that pay on that post. Let's say I bought the products I love this brand all that can stuff goes a long way to helping conversion Top of funnel so that's just something to think about in terms of building your Social proofing at different parts of your funnel. The other thing that we do actually is We group our ads as well so for example here we've created multiple copies of the ad set in one campaign and We've set a budget limit per ad set as well So right now each of these ad sets have a limit of five dollars and we have an overall CBO campaign limit So that when we're running the ads within each of these audiences because we have I don't know Maybe 20 or 30 ads maybe even more that needs social proofing. We split them into five ads per ad set So it's literally a dollar a day So five ads per ad set each ad set has a spend cap of five dollars and then forever every New batch of ads would create beyond five we create a new ad set and keep duplicating it that way the social proofing gets shared fairly Across those different ad sets as well. Actually the cool thing is that actually it's actually generating a pretty good return on ad spend as well because if you think about it the warm audiences are People that have shown a good interest.
